page: Microsoft Azure D2pds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DPDSv5 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at a stable frequency of 3.0 GHz. The server configuration includes 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 2 vCPUs. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled in the form of a 77 GB local disk, and the instance is premium storage capable. The virtualization layer is managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance but overall weak multi-core and memory capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated as average at 1822.18, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also achieve average ratings. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core at 3612.77 and Geekbench multi-core at 2008.0, fall into the weak category. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 140.69 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 15.78 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores are also poor, reflecting the limitations of this entry-level ARM architecture.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2pds_v5 offers a low-cost entry point into ARM-based cloud computing, trading off high-performance execution for dedicated physical resources at a lower hardware class. Given its weak multi-threaded performance, poor database operations, and low LLM inference speeds, this server is not suited for heavy computational tasks, large databases, or AI workloads. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, simple web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, and basic development or testing environments that benefit from dedicated ARM cores and local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D2pds_v5 is an ARM-based general-purpose virtual machine from the DPDSv5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) clocked at 3.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 77 GB local disk. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, such as a stress-ng single-core score of 1822.18, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The server is a cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads that do not require high memory throughput or heavy multi-threaded processing. It is best suited for basic web serving, single-threaded tasks, and ARM-based development or testing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2pds_v5 is an ARM-based general-purpose VM featuring 2 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 77 GB local storage, suitable for lightweight workloads and testing.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2pds_v5 is a general-purpose ARM VM with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 77 GB local storage, optimized for basic single-threaded tasks.
tagline: An entry-level ARM-based virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores and local storage for lightweight, single-threaded utility workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Features 2 dedicated physical cores with no hyperthreading
Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and a 77 GB local storage disk
Delivers average single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core scaling
Best suited for lightweight web serving, basic testing, and ARM development
